reason: The statement applies because King Josiah is described in the text as a leader who makes significant reforms by removing idolatry, destroying pagan altars, enforcing the laws of the covenant, and purging the land of abominations. These actions were aimed at turning the people back to the worship of the LORD and following the commandments, which would have improved the lives of the regular people and less fortunate by restoring proper worship and adherence to the laws.
